title: Retire the `done:` subtask field + add migration tooling
description: `done:` is a vestigial subtask frontmatter field — the loader reads `state:` first and only falls back to `done` for pre-4-state files, and the displayed completion is re-derived from `state`, so `done:` is inert whenever `state:` is present. Remove it everywhere as if it never existed (skill + framework code), and stand up a reusable, self-documenting migration-script convention (a `migration/` folder of dated Python one-off scripts) with `done:`→`state:` as the first migration.
author: sidhantha
